0.name: Prof. Candice
0.text: In your pre-assessment, you were asked what Inertia is.

2.name: Classmate
2.text: It refers to the object to remain at rest or keep moving once it is in motion!

3.name: Prof. Candice
3.text: Exactly! Imagine using a blower to apply a constant force to accelerate a ball.

4.name: Prof. Candice
4.text: Meanwhile, another person is also using another blower to apply an equal and constant force opposite to your direction.

5.name: Prof. Candice
5.text: Thus, the opposing force together with your force will cause the ball to..?

6.name: Classmate
6.text: The ball will continue to move between opposing directions but remain steady due to the equal and constant force on opposing sides.

8.name: Prof. Candice
8.text: Once the projectile is in flight, it doesn't experience forward force.

9.name: Prof. Candice
9.text: But it flies because of the Law's statement that an object in motion tends to stay in motion.

10.name: Prof. Candice
10.text: The reason why objects that we observe stop, is because there are different forms of forces that make them stop

11.name: Prof. Candice
11.text: For example, the catapult stops because of gravitational force.

12.name: Prof. Candice
12.text: It pulls the projectile to the ground transferring its kinetic energy to the impact.

13.name: Prof. Candice
13.text: Other factors could also contribute to this such as air resistance or friction.

14.name: Prof. Candice
14.text: Why don't we have Colin up here and show it us by pushing this large box?

16.name: Prof. Candice
16.text: You might need to shove this with [pulse]FULL FORCE[/pulse]!
